topic: Why cholesterol isn't the villain you think it is
headline: Cholesterol: Your Body's Misunderstood Building Block
caption:
Here's something that might surprise you: your body actually NEEDS cholesterol to survive.

I know, I know—we've been told for years that cholesterol is the enemy. But here's the full picture:

Your body uses cholesterol to build every single cell membrane, produce hormones like testosterone and estrogen, make vitamin D when sunlight hits your skin, and create bile acids that help you digest fats.

In fact, your liver produces about 80% of your cholesterol because it's that important!

So what's the real issue? It's not cholesterol itself—it's the BALANCE and TYPE that matters. Think of LDL as the delivery truck (can cause problems when oxidized or excessive) and HDL as the cleanup crew (helps remove excess).

The takeaway? Stop demonizing cholesterol completely. Instead, focus on:
• Eating whole foods
• Regular movement
• Managing stress
• Getting quality sleep

Your body is smarter than you think. Sometimes we just need to support it better, not wage war against natural processes.

#CholesterolMyth #HeartHealth #MedicalEducation #DoctorExplains #HealthyLiving
